refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Correlate with telemetry data (vehicle speed, acceleration, operator input latency, safety system activation events) to isolate performance bottlenecks.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2.A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical response times and operator control loop latency through hardware tuning and network QoS prioritization; monitor cycle time via telemetry and adjust operational parameters (speed profiles, safety thresholds) to meet p95 target.
DP-2.B: Implement a closed-loop cycle time controller that dynamically adjusts vehicle speed setpoints and safety constraint margins based on real-time telemetry feedback to maintain p95 ≤ CT_target.

DP-2.C: Decompose cycle time into load, travel, and dump sub-cycles; allocate timing budgets to each; design vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems to meet allocated budgets independently; integrate and validate p95 performance.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2.C: Decompose cycle time into load, travel, and dump sub-cycles; allocate timing budgets to each; design vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems to meet allocated budgets independently; integrate and validate p95 performance.

potential_sources: Safety constraint activation (e.g., emergency stop) may couple dump cycle timing to safety services response latency.
Teleoperation latency couples travel cycle timing to network link quality and tele-operator responsiveness.
Vehicle hydraulic response couples all sub-cycles to vehicle design parameters (pump capacity, valve response, load mass).
coupling_check.notes: DP-2.C mitigates coupling by explicit budget allocation and integration testing. Residual couplings are managed via telemetry monitoring and operational parameter tuning (DP-2.A fallback). Safety criticality (high) requires that safety constraint overhead is quantified and budgeted explicitly.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and stable; risk: vague or changing targets invalidate budget allocation.

Assumption: Vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems can be designed and tested independently within allocated budgets; risk: integration reveals unforeseen couplings (e.g., safety system latency exceeds budget).
Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure is reliable and provides sub-cycle timing resolution; risk: telemetry gaps or jitter prevent accurate p95 measurement.

Assumption: Operator skill and site conditions are within the agreed duty cycle envelope; risk: out-of-envelope operation (e.g., extreme terrain, inexperienced operator) degrades cycle time unpredictably.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2.C to explicitly include: (1) formal duty cycle specification (load mass range, terrain type, operator skill level); (2) timing budget allocation table (load, travel, dump, safety overhead); (3) telemetry schema for cycle time verification; (4) integration test plan with p95 acceptance criteria. Add contingency: if integration testing reveals budget overruns, escalate to DP-2.A (parameter tuning) or DP-2.B (closed-loop control).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level integration boundary encompassing vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services subsystems. Responsible for cycle time KPI achievement through budget allocation, integration testing, and telemetry-based performance monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Cycle time telemetry (load/travel/dump sub-cycle durations, operator input latency, safety intervention events, vehicle state) streamed to site manager and system engineer for KPI verification and diagnostics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Duty cycle specification (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, operator skill level, CT_target) provided by site manager to configure vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety constraint violations or emergency conditions reported to safety manager; may trigger cycle time overhead (e.g., speed reduction, emergency stop).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Tele-operator commands and vehicle state feedback exchanged via network; latency on this link directly impacts travel cycle timing.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary. Cycle time KPI is a system-level outcome that requires coordination across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. Containment is high because all sub-systems and their interfaces are within NGHE Solution scope. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry reporting and tasking input cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns load and dump sub-cycle timing (hydraulic response, mechanical actuation). Travel cycle timing is shared with teleoperation (operator command latency) and safety services (speed constraints). Containment is moderate because vehicle cannot independently achieve p95 cycle time without teleoperation and safety coordination.

Teleoperation owns operator command latency and network link quality, which directly impact travel cycle timing. However, travel cycle also depends on vehicle dynamics and safety constraints. Containment is low because teleoperation is one of three coupled factors affecting travel timing.

Safety services impose constraints (speed limits, emergency stops) that affect all sub-cycles. Safety overhead is diffused across load, travel, and dump cycles. Containment is low because safety is a cross-cutting concern that couples to vehicle and teleoperation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI is a system-level performance outcome that cannot be owned by a single subsystem (vehicle, teleoperation, or safety services). NGHE Solution must own the integration responsibility: defining timing budgets, allocating them to subsystems, integrating and testing, and monitoring telemetry to verify p95 achievement. Vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services own their respective sub-cycle or constraint contributions.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution ownership is diffuse if budget allocation and integration testing responsibilities are not explicitly assigned to a single team (e.g., system engineering or integration engineering).
Risk: Teleoperation latency is partially owned by external network infrastructure (site network, ISP); NGHE Solution must define acceptable latency bounds and verify network QoS.
Risk: Safety services may impose timing overhead that exceeds budget; escalation path and contingency (e.g., DP-2.A parameter tuning) must be pre-planned.
Risk: Duty cycle specification (tasking input) may be ambiguous or change; NGHE Solution must establish formal change control and re-baseline timing budgets if duty cycle changes.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish a Cycle Time Integration Team (CTIT) within NGHE Solution ownership to: (1) formalize duty cycle and CT_target; (2) allocate timing budgets to vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services; (3) define telemetry schema and acceptance criteria; (4) execute integration testing and p95 verification; (5) manage budget overruns via escalation to DP-2.A or DP-2.B. Assign explicit ownership of network QoS (teleoperation latency) to site infrastructure or teleoperation module with SLA.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-2.C (budget allocation and integration testing) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve p95 ≤ CT_target via decomposition and subsystem coordination). The DP is solution-neutral (does not prescribe specific vehicle speed profiles or safety thresholds) and allows flexibility in implementation. Coupling check identifies residual couplings (safety overhead, teleoperation latency, vehicle dynamics) but DP-2.C mitigates them via explicit budgeting and integration testing.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Cycle time KPI ownership is distributed across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services modules. NGHE Solution must establish clear integration governance and escalation paths.
Diffusion Risk 2: Telemetry interface (out) requires coordination across all subsystems to collect and aggregate sub-cycle timing data. Telemetry schema must be standardized.
Diffusion Risk 3: Tasking interface (in) requires duty cycle specification to be formally defined and communicated to all subsystems. Change control is critical.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for cycle time KPI ownership because it encompasses all subsystems and their interfaces. However, the actual implementation of DP-2.C (budget allocation, integration testing, telemetry monitoring) must be assigned to a specific team or module within NGHE Solution (e.g., System Engineering or Integration Engineering). This team does not appear in the available modules list and should be created or explicitly designated.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via telemetry-based measurement of p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Telemetry interface (out) supports this. Acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target) are measurable and objective.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear at the system level (NGHE Solution owns KPI) but requires further decomposition at the subsystem level (vehicle, teleoperation, safety services own sub-cycle budgets). Sub-FR allocation to subsystems should be completed in the next decomposition level.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Formalize duty cycle specification (tasking input) with explicit ranges for load mass, travel distance, terrain type, operator skill level, and environmental conditions. Document as a baseline requirement.

Revision 2: Create a Cycle Time Integration Team (CTIT) within NGHE Solution to own DP-2.C implementation. Assign explicit responsibilities for budget allocation, integration testing, and telemetry monitoring.

Revision 3: Define telemetry schema for cycle time verification, including sub-cycle timestamps, latency events, safety interventions, and vehicle state. Establish data collection and analysis procedures.
Revision 4: Establish escalation path and contingency plan if integration testing reveals budget overruns. Pre-plan fallback to DP-2.A (parameter tuning) or DP-2.B (closed-loop control).
Revision 5: Define network QoS requirements for teleoperation latency and assign ownership (teleoperation module or site infrastructure) with SLA.

decision.justification: The refined FR and selected DP are sound and pass the FR-DP gate. However, DP-2.C requires decomposition into sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) to allocate cycle time budgets to vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services subsystems. The IM assignment (NGHE Solution) is correct at the system level but requires further refinement at the subsystem level. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify ownership, interfaces, and integration responsibilities.
decision.justification_detail: The high safety criticality of this requirement and the distributed nature of cycle time KPI (coupled across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services) necessitate explicit budget allocation and integration testing. The next level of decomposition will map sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) to subsystem DPs and IMs, establishing clear ownership and interface contracts.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets (load_budget, travel_budget, dump_budget).
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve travel cycle timing performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification.

assumptions: Assumption A1: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and stable throughout the project lifecycle.
Assumption A2: Vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems can be designed and tested independently within allocated timing budgets.
Assumption A3: Telemetry infrastructure is reliable and provides sub-cycle timing resolution (≤100 ms granularity).
Assumption A4: Operator skill and site conditions remain within the agreed duty cycle envelope during field deployment.
Assumption A5: Network latency for teleoperation is predictable and can be budgeted (e.g., ≤200 ms round-trip).
Assumption A6: Safety constraint overhead (speed reduction, emergency stop latency) can be quantified and budgeted.
open_risks: Risk R1: Vague or changing CT_target invalidates timing budget allocation; mitigation: formalize duty cycle specification and establish change control.
Risk R2: Integration testing reveals unforeseen couplings (e.g., safety system latency exceeds budget); mitigation: early integration testing and contingency planning (DP-2.A or DP-2.B).
Risk R3: Telemetry infrastructure is unreliable or lacks timing resolution; mitigation: validate telemetry system early in development.
Risk R4: Operator skill or site conditions exceed duty cycle envelope; mitigation: operator training and site assessment procedures.
Risk R5: Network latency is unpredictable or exceeds budget; mitigation: network QoS provisioning and fallback to local autonomy (reduced tele-operation dependency).
Risk R6: Safety constraint overhead exceeds budget, making p95 target unachievable; mitigation: early safety analysis and contingency planning.
evidence_needed: Evidence E1: Formal duty cycle specification document (load mass range, travel distance, terrain type, operator skill level, environmental conditions, CT_target).
Evidence E2: Timing budget allocation table (load_budget, travel_budget, dump_budget, safety_overhead_budget, latency_budget).
Evidence E3: Telemetry schema and data collection procedures for cycle time verification.
Evidence E4: Vehicle sub-cycle timing analysis (load, travel, dump) with deterministic bounds.
Evidence E5: Teleoperation latency measurement and network QoS requirements.
Evidence E6: Safety constraint overhead analysis (speed reduction, emergency stop latency impact on cycle time).
Evidence E7: Integration test plan with p95 acceptance criteria and ≥100 representative cycles.
Evidence E8: Contingency plan for budget overruns (escalation to DP-2.A or DP-2.B).
